mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains at or near zero while CPI inflation accelerates above 8% year-over-year, signaling monetary policy lag. Real rates turn deeply negative, compressing equity risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 returns as investors rotate into real assets and equities as inflation hedges.
evidence_refs: fact:As of April 2022, FEDFUNDS=0.2% and CPIAUCSL=287.708 (8.5% YoY)
regime_hint: monetary policy lag during inflation surge
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window during periods meeting trigger criteria.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate remains below 4% while payroll employment growth decelerates month-over-month, signaling labor market tightness without acceleration. This tight labor market supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive forward equity returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:As of April 2022, UNRATE=3.6% and PAYEMS=150925k
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market tightness with moderating hiring
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 1.5%) when trigger is active, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth decelerates sharply (month-over-month growth < 0.2%) while housing starts fall below 1.5 million annualized units, signaling demand destruction across consumer and housing sectors. This demand weakness forces equity repricing downward as forward earnings expectations compress over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:As of April 2022, RSAFS=665726M and HOUST=1769k
regime_hint: demand contraction phase with housing and consumption weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.03 (i.e., signal fails to predict negative returns).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines month-over-month for two consecutive months, signaling supply-side contraction and potential stagflation. Equity markets reprice downward as stagflation scenarios become more probable, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as volatility spikes.
candidate_trigger: month-over-month INDPRO decline > 0.5% for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC remains above -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains positive and above 2% annualized, with no recession signal in the prior quarter. This sustained demand backdrop supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as a baseline always-on signal.
evidence_refs: fact:As of Q4 2021 (latest available), GDPC1=19806.29B (positive growth)
regime_hint: any regime with positive GDP growth
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly positive (< 1.0%) during periods of positive GDP growth, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate rises above 1.0% in a single month (indicating aggressive tightening cycle initiation), while CPI remains elevated above 7%. This policy shock triggers equity repricing as discount rates rise and growth expectations compress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary policy tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.04 (fails to predict negative returns).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points in a single month, signaling rapid labor market deterioration. This shock triggers equity repricing as recession probability increases and forward earnings expectations decline, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market shock or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 70% of triggered instances, or Rank IC remains above -0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index grows month-over-month while payroll employment expands by > 200k, signaling robust supply-side capacity expansion and labor productivity gains. This supply strength supports profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: month-over-month INDPRO growth > 0.3% AND month-over-month PAYEMS growth > 200k
evidence_refs: fact:As of April 2022, INDPRO=104.5853 and PAYEMS=150925k
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with strong labor productivity
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly positive (< 1.5%) when trigger is active, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.
